title: Python codegen-owned primitive executor migration

execplan_profile.projection_rule: canonical_core is authoritative for intent, scope, next action, proof, continuation, and closeout; legacy fields remain compatibility projections.

canonical_core.hard_constraints: Keep scope bounded to the promoted TODO item and its stated touched paths.
canonical_core.agent_may_decide: Bounded decomposition, touched-path narrowing, validation tightening, and plan-local residue routing.
canonical_core.escalate_when: A better-looking fix changes the requested outcome, owned surface, time horizon, or meaningful validation story.
canonical_core.next_action: Generic deterministic CLI-visible behavior over files, JSON, TOML, Markdown sections, schemas, templates, managed payloads, and JSON text emission moves from module-local runtime helpers into codegen-owned primitive executors where practical.
completion_criteria: Primitive conformance and generated Python black-box conformance prove representative operations execute through codegen-owned primitives without importing module-local CLI/runtime internals except explicitly inventoried package-specific judgment.

non_goals: Leave adjacent backlog or follow-on work out of this plan.

intent.constraints: Keep scope bounded to the promoted TODO item and its stated touched paths.
intent.latitude: Bounded decomposition, touched-path narrowing, validation tightening, and plan-local residue routing.

execution.next_step: Generic deterministic CLI-visible behavior over files, JSON, TOML, Markdown sections, schemas, templates, managed payloads, and JSON text emission moves from module-local runtime helpers into codegen-owned primitive executors where practical.
execution.proof: Primitive conformance tests plus generated Python conformance.

invariants: Preserve the planning contract and keep the work bounded to this plan.

iterative_follow_through.what this slice enabled: Planning and Memory verify-payload, direct planning installer handlers, lower-risk memory report/sync installer handlers, and memory note creation now execute through generated operation IR using a codegen-owned checked-in python.function.call bridge instead of generated module-local function-call handlers.
iterative_follow_through.intentionally deferred: Memory lifecycle installer function-call handlers remain future slices. Runtime-primitives handlers remain explicit domain-runtime wrappers until they can be decomposed into portable primitive IR.
iterative_follow_through.discovered implications: python.function.call must stay classified as an explicit external runtime bridge and must not be counted as portable shared target-executor coverage.
iterative_follow_through.validation still needed: Continue migrating representative module-local handlers to codegen-owned primitives and rerun generated Python/Docker conformance for each slice.
iterative_follow_through.next likely slice: Migrate high-risk memory lifecycle handlers as a separately proven slice, or classify them as retained domain-runtime wrappers if proof shows the bridge is the wrong boundary.

intent_interpretation.chosen concrete what: Generic deterministic CLI-visible behavior over files, JSON, TOML, Markdown sections, schemas, templates, managed payloads, and JSON text emission moves from module-local runtime helpers into codegen-owned primitive executors where practical.
intent_interpretation.review guidance: Confirm the scaffolded plan still matches the promoted item before broad implementation.

execution_bounds.max changed files: About 6 files for the primitive conformance baseline and planning schema-drift fix before the next migration slice.
execution_bounds.required validation commands: uv run pytest tests/test_command_generation_primitive_executor.py packages/planning/tests/test_promote.py -q; uv run python scripts/check/check_generated_command_packages.py --python-conformance; make test-workspace; make lint-planning.
execution_bounds.ask-before-refactor threshold: Ask before broadening beyond the promoted item.
execution_bounds.stop before touching: Unrelated backlog, adjacent modules, or canonical contracts not named by this plan.

execution_run.what happened: Added python.function.call to the codegen primitive executor and registry as a checked-in external runtime bridge, migrated planning.verify-payload.report and memory.verify-payload.report to use it, migrated the remaining direct planning installer function-call handlers and lower-risk memory report/sync installer function-call handlers to operation IR, removed the corresponding generated planning/memory handler paths, regenerated command packages/adapters, and tightened missing value binding failures.
execution_run.scope touched: packages/command-generation/src/command_generation/primitive_executor.py; tests/test_command_generation_primitive_executor.py; packages/planning/src/repo_planning_bootstrap/contracts/operations; packages/memory/src/repo_memory_bootstrap/contracts/operations/memory.verify-payload.report.json; src/agentic_workspace/contracts/operation_primitives.json; src/agentic_workspace/contracts/command_adapter_generation.json; src/agentic_workspace/contracts/command_package_ir.json; generated/python/planning-cli; generated/python/memory-cli; generated/typescript/planning-cli; generated/typescript/memory-cli; active execplan metadata.
execution_run.changed surfaces: Codegen primitive executor; operation primitive registry; planning operation IR for direct installer calls; memory verify-payload operation IR; command adapter/package metadata; generated planning/memory Python and TypeScript projections; focused primitive conformance tests.

finished_run_review.misinterpretation risk: medium if python.function.call is mistaken for portable TypeScript-ready primitive coverage; registry classifies it as external-adapter to prevent that. High-risk lifecycle calls were migrated only for planning after full generated/Docker proof.
finished_run_review.follow-on decision: continue current lane with memory lifecycle direct function-call handlers or explicit retention classification

delegation_outcome_feedback.quality concern: generic dynamic import primitives must remain checked-in-contract-only, not user-input execution, and should stay external-adapter rather than portable target-executor coverage
